title: [lichen_l2.c:263-268] TX/RX buffers are not cache-line aligned
description:
Completeness: Performance/DMA alignment category.

Static buffers at lines 263-268:
- tx_ipv6_buf[240]
- tx_frame_buf[255]
- rx_ipv6_buf[240]

These are used with hardware drivers that may use DMA. On Cortex-M with cache (M7, M33), non-cache-aligned DMA buffers can cause:
1. Cache coherency issues if CPU caches overlap DMA buffer
2. False sharing with adjacent variables

Most Cortex-M0/M3/M4 have no cache, so this is not currently an issue. But portability to M7 (e.g., STM32H7) would require:
- __attribute__((aligned(32))) or K_HEAP_DEFINE with alignment
- Cache maintenance operations before/after DMA

The current code works because the LoRa driver abstracts DMA details. But if porting to a platform with CPU cache, review buffer alignment.

This is P4 informational for future portability.
close_reason: Added PORTABILITY NOTE comment about cache-line alignment for DMA on Cortex-M7/M33
